dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 4 responding for a possible cardiac arrest at the [COMPANY_NAME] pre-op unit.
Hi, I'm Paramedic Mike. I'm with the fire department. Can you tell me what happened?
She had IV contrast for a procedure and then developed a rash and trouble breathing.
Ma'am, can you open your eyes and tell me your name?
She's opening her eyes to pain and localizing to the right, but no verbal response.
Eyes open slowly and require continuous stimulation to stay open.
After Epi administration, she responded to her name and knew the day was Thursday-oriented to time only.
I think it's Thursday. I don't know where I am.
Airway is patent. [COMPANY_NAME] staff are managing with BVM. No signs of obstruction.
Breathing is 38, labored, and shallow. Work of breathing is increased.
Pulse is 130, strong and regular. Blood pressure is 90/40.
Respiratory rate 38, labored with equal depth bilaterally.
Heart rate is 130 BPM, regular rhythm.
Blood pressure reading is 90/40 mmHg.
No glucose reading due to suspected anaphylaxis and no IV access for testing.
Hives present on face and chest. No signs of trauma.
First known allergic reaction occurred after IV contrast was given for a procedure.
Eyes are PERRL. Trachea midline. No JVD. Head and neck without trauma.
Chest wall is normal with no tenderness. Breath sounds diminished in the bases, both fields.
Abdomen is soft, non-tender, without distension or guarding.
Pelvis is intact, no movement tenderness or instability noted.
Oxygen saturation is 88% on room air.
0.3 mg of epinephrine administered IM. [COMPANY_NAME] staff gave 50 mg IV Benadryl.
[COMPANY_NAME] staff administered Benadryl but it did not fully relieve the breathing trouble.
4-lead ECG applied. Rhythm is noted as sinus tachy.
Initiated treatment under anaphylaxis protocol for acute allergic reaction.
O2 is up to 89%, BP is 100/48, and heart rate is 117.
CPAP is initiated with FiO2 30% and PEEP 5.
10 mg dexamethasone given IV for second-line steroid support.
EKG confirms sinus tachycardia. No QRS widening or ST changes.
BP now at 103/57, SpO2 at 94%. Respiratory rate is 36.
Radioed [COMPANY_NAME] ED for bed 16 preparation.
Five-person lift used to move from pre-op table to stretcher.
Patient secured in semi-Fowler's position via five straps and two raised rails.
Skin is pale but stable; minimal erythema and hives remain.
Vital signs slightly improved. Continued tachycardia, 105 BPM, with tachypnea, 36 RPM.
Reconfirming under allergic reaction protocol. ED alerted via pre-transmission notes.
All extensor and flexor movements noted. Decreased tonicity in both arms and legs.
No sensory deficit reported. Patient is cooperative with prompting.
IV line in right arm. All vessels patent without guarding or instability.
CPAP with adequate seal and oxygen was used without distress.
No acute changes observed on 12-lead. EKG still shows tach but no LDSI.
Twelve-lead obtained for field transmission and ED reference.
36 breaths per minute, depth improved slightly from pre-CPAP.
Patient transported under full support. ED aware via pre-hospital transmission.
Patient moved to ED bed 16 with CPAP and monitoring continued.
All signs and systems under control during transport, no drop in vitals.
94% O2, 105/58 BP. Heart rate remains 105, regular rhythm.
Skin remains pale with hives and erythema. No trauma noted.
When was the last time she had food or fluids before this event?
Meal was several hours ago. Since then, only IV fluids given.
Verbal response erratic but motor response follows commands inconsistently.
EKG remains stable. O2 saturation consistent at 94%.
12-lead transmitted. ED personnel received and reviewed field impressions.
Patient now transferred on hospital bed with CPAP continuted by ED staff.
No trauma to spine or pelvis found or reported during transport.
CPAP maintained. EMS personnel monitoring for seal and effectiveness.
No significant pain reported or observed during patient move.
Protocol cleared by ED during handover. All steps follow [COMPANY_NAME] documentation.
Patient transferred to ED with full equipment and a stable vital profile.
Provided full care report to receiving nurse, Jennifer Farah.
All patient details taken and fields cross-checked with med history and [COMPANY_NAME] staff.
EMS checks completed. All equipment cleared from scene.
EMD status confirmed as cleared from scene. No further emergency action by EMS.
[COMPANY_NAME] confirmed as receiving facility. Call type closed for cardiac arrest/death.
